summary:
Streptococcus faecalis, Salmonella typhi, and Pseudomonas aeruginosa are all associated with infectious endocarditis based on available references. In contrast, *Staphylococcus albus* lacks any direct or indirect link to endocarditis among these pathogens, making it the most improbable causative organism from this list.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only two sentences.)

The agent identifies that *S. albus* has no known connection to infective endocarditis within provided literature, unlike other organisms such as *Enterococcus faecalis*, *Salmonella typhi*, and *Pseudomonas aeruginosa*. Therefore, due to its lack of documentation even incidentally linking it to endocarditis, it is considered the least probable pathogen for causing this condition.
